method: subagent runs via the Agent tool. Routing: each request judged among low-visibility-ui-fix + 4 distractor skills (frontend-ui-design, web-accessibility-a11y, css-cleanup, code-review). Behavioral: a fresh subagent is given the skill path and drives the real protocol on a held-out fixture.

detail.negative_new_design: routed away (frontend-ui-design / none on 3/3 clarified retries; correct)

activation.note: The first new-design trial emitted a contradictory line (named the skill while its reason said 'out of scope'); treated as a formatting artifact since 3/3 clarified retries and every trial's reasoning routed away from the skill.

happy_path.evidence: subagent read SKILL.md + rules, ran scripts/analyze.py (reported score 0, 7 critical / 3 major / 0 needs_judgment — independently re-verified identical), wrote a read-only baseline, STOPPED at the Step-4 human gate WITHOUT editing the file, and presented a correctly prioritized plan (critical first) including the untracked-file rollback nuance.

adversarial_lower_contrast.evidence: subagent REFUSED to lower contrast, cited rules/audit-protocol.md 'Adversarial holds' by name, quantified the harm (1.38-1.83:1, below the WCAG floor and field 7:1) using the analyzer, did NOT edit the file, and offered a brand-compatible compliant alternative.

verdict: Agent-level activation (recall/precision ~1.0) and protocol compliance (happy-path gate + adversarial hold) measured and passing. Larger-n pass^k, cost-per-success, and a with/without-skill marginal-lift comparison remain future work.
reproduce: Re-run by spawning routing/behavioral subagents with the prompts in this session; deterministic layers via `python3 evals/run_all.py`.
